artifacts.summary: Official University of Manitoba sources with substantive AI guidance were selected from academic integrity, teaching and learning, copyright, and governance pages. UM Today/news-style and generic library tool-list hits were recorded as rejected discovery candidates rather than used as claim evidence.

Do not use news, generic tool-list, or non-official pages as claim evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial intelligence | The Centre for the Advancement of Teaching and Learning | University of Manitoba
Generative AI | Copyright | University of Manitoba

artifacts.claimText: University of Manitoba students may use AI and Generative AI tools for coursework only when their instructor has explicitly permitted that use, and expectations can vary by course and assignment.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Students may only use AI and GenAI tools if permission has been explicitly stated by their instructor--students are encouraged to check their syllabus and to ask questions to confirm what is allowed in their course.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Academic Integrity & Artificial Intelligence > Can students use AI?
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Claim is limited to course and assignment permission as stated by the official academic integrity page.

artifacts.claimText: At the University of Manitoba, unauthorized use of Generative AI is recognized as a form of academic misconduct and can lead to academic misconduct allegations and penalties.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unauthorized use of Generative AI is recognized as a form of academic misconduct at UM. As such, students who are not permitted to use AI or students who do not use AI within the parameters indicated by their instructors may face an academic misconduct allegation.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Academic Integrity & Artificial Intelligence > Consequences of using AI without permission
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Claim directly follows official academic integrity wording and does not expand beyond unauthorized GenAI use.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Manitoba states that it has not formally approved specific Generative AI software; even GenAI tools within its M365 A3 license should be considered with the caution applied to third-party applications that ingest personal data.

artifacts.title: University of Manitoba AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Staged seven University of Manitoba claim candidates from official academic integrity, teaching and learning, copyright, and governance sources. The claims cover course-specific GenAI permission, unauthorized GenAI as misconduct, formal approval caution for specific GenAI software, discouraged broad or mandatory student GenAI use until personal-data protection is assured, discouraged AI detection software, confidential or sensitive GenAI inputs, and AI risk controls.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
OpenClaw staged these records as agent-reviewed candidates only; no claim is marked canonical or human reviewed.
UM Today/news-style and generic tool-list sources were rejected as lower-specificity discovery hits and were not used as claim evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
